title: NNN and STNL Property Sourcing
name: NNN and STNL Property Sourcing
metaTitle: NNN and STNL Property Sourcing in San Antonio, TX
metaDescription: Credit-tenant net-lease sourcing along San Antonio's I-35 and I-10 corridors, structured for straightforward 1031 identification and debt replacement.

sections.html: Triple-net and single-tenant net-lease sourcing for a San Antonio exchange focuses on freestanding pads leased to national or regional operators along the metro's primary retail corridors. These assets are attractive replacement candidates because the lease itself, not the building, carries most of the operating risk, which simplifies diligence inside a compressed identification window, especially for investors moving out of a more management-intensive property type.

sections.heading: I-35 and I-10 Retail Pad Corridors
sections.html:
Freestanding net-lease pads in San Antonio concentrate along the I-35 corridor running north toward New Braunfels and along I-10 through the west and northwest submarkets, where daily traffic counts and rooftop growth support quick-service, pharmacy, and auto-service operators. Pad sites near these corridors tend to hold value even when the surrounding retail center around them changes tenants.

Downtown redevelopment activity near the Pearl has also produced smaller-format net-lease opportunities aimed at a more urban, walkable customer base, which is a different risk and rent-growth profile than a suburban I-35 pad and should be underwritten separately.

sections.heading: Credit Tenant and Lease Guarantee Review
sections.html:
The value of a net-lease replacement property in San Antonio rests almost entirely on tenant credit and the strength of the lease guarantee, so diligence starts with the guarantor entity, not the real estate. A corporate-guaranteed lease from a national operator behaves very differently in a downturn than a franchisee-guaranteed lease at the same address.

  • Guarantor entity and corporate versus franchisee structure
  • Remaining primary lease term and renewal option pricing
  • Rent escalation schedule and reimbursement structure
  • Landlord responsibilities carved out of the net lease
  • Co-tenancy or exclusivity clauses affecting future re-leasing

sections.heading: Debt Replacement on Single-Tenant Assets
sections.html:
Lenders size net-lease debt heavily around remaining lease term and tenant credit rating, which means a San Antonio pad with a short lease runway may not support the loan amount an exchange investor needs to replace the debt retired on the relinquished property. Confirming achievable proceeds before identification avoids a late scramble to fill a debt gap.

A sale-leaseback pad, where the operator sold the real estate and signed a fresh long-term lease back to itself, often carries a longer remaining term and cleaner lease structure than an older pad that has already been renewed once or twice, which can matter when comparing loan proceeds across San Antonio candidates.

sections.heading: Identification Timing for Fast-Moving Pad Sites
sections.html:
Well-located net-lease pads along San Antonio's growth corridors often go under contract quickly, so identification lists frequently name a signed contract plus one or two backup candidates rather than open listings. That sequencing keeps the exchange on track if financing or title issues delay the lead property.

Brokers who specialize in net-lease product across San Antonio typically maintain relationships with the regional operators expanding into the metro, which can surface a pad site before it is broadly marketed and give the investor more time to complete diligence within the identification window.

sections.heading: Ground Lease and Fee-Simple Distinctions
sections.html:
Some net-lease pad sites in San Antonio are sold subject to an underlying ground lease rather than in fee simple, which changes both the diligence checklist and the financing picture. A ground lease with a long remaining term and reasonable rent escalations can still make a defensible replacement property, but the ground lease terms need review with the same rigor as the tenant lease itself.

Fee-simple pads remain the more common structure across San Antonio's suburban corridors, but ground-leased sites show up more often in tighter, higher-traffic locations where the underlying landowner chose not to sell the dirt outright.

Ground lease escalation schedules and remaining term should be checked against the tenant lease's own term as well, since a ground lease expiring before the tenant lease creates a mismatch that can complicate both financing and future resale of a San Antonio pad site.

faqs.q: What makes a net-lease pad a straightforward 1031 replacement candidate in San Antonio?
faqs.a: Because the tenant covers most operating expenses under the lease, diligence centers on lease terms and guarantor credit rather than building systems, which shortens the review cycle compared to multi-tenant retail or multifamily.

faqs.q: Is a corporate-guaranteed lease always stronger than a franchisee-guaranteed lease?
faqs.a: Generally yes, since a corporate guarantee ties performance to a larger balance sheet, but some franchisee groups in the San Antonio market operate at scale with strong financials, so the guarantor's actual financial statements should be reviewed rather than assumed from the brand name alone.

faqs.q: Why does remaining lease term matter for financing a net-lease replacement property?
faqs.a: Lenders size loan proceeds around the income stream's durability, and a lease with only a few years remaining typically supports less debt than one with a long primary term or well-structured renewal options, which affects whether the investor can replace enough debt to avoid boot. A sale-leaseback structure with a freshly signed long-term lease can address this concern directly.

faqs.q: Do downtown net-lease sites near the Pearl underwrite the same as suburban I-35 pads?
faqs.a: No. Urban, walkable net-lease sites depend more on foot traffic and neighborhood density, while suburban corridor pads depend on drive-by traffic counts and rooftop growth, so the two should be evaluated on separate assumptions even within the same exchange search. Financing terms for each category can also differ meaningfully.

faqs.q: What happens if a net-lease pad under contract falls through during the 45-day window?
faqs.a: A properly structured identification list under the three-property rule names backup candidates in advance, so the investor can pivot to an alternative property without missing the identification deadline. Rebuilding a list from scratch after the deadline has passed is generally not possible.
